    <h1 {...{
      "id": "brand-concierge-implementation-guide-ios"
    }}>{`Brand Concierge Implementation Guide (iOS)`}</h1>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge UI is presented in two steps:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Enable UI presentation`}</strong>{` by wrapping your SwiftUI root with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Concierge.wrap(...)`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Open the chat`}</strong>{` by calling `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Concierge.show(...)`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Internally, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.show(...)`}</inlineCode>{` dispatches an event in the Adobe Experience Platform Mobile SDK that the Concierge extension handles to build a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeConfiguration`}</inlineCode>{`, then the SwiftUI overlay presents `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ChatView`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "prerequisites"
    }}>{`Prerequisites`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "required-sdk-modules"
    }}>{`Required SDK modules`}</h3>
    <p>{`Your app needs the following Experience Platform SDKs to be available and registered:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPCore`}</strong>{` (MobileCore; Configuration shared state from `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`configureWith(appId:)`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPEdgeIdentity`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPBrandConcierge`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "ios-version"
    }}>{`iOS version`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Minimum iOS 15.0+`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "permissions-for-speech-to-text-optional"
    }}>{`Permissions for speech to text (optional)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Speech to text uses iOS Speech and microphone APIs. Add these to your app `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Info.plist`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`}</inlineCode></strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`}</inlineCode></strong></li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The SDK handles permission requests internally when the user taps the microphone button; no additional permission-request code is required from the host app.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "installation"
    }}>{`Installation`}</h2>
    <p>{`Add Brand Concierge alongside the other AEP SDK extensions using Swift Package Manager, CocoaPods, or by adding the XCFramework directly.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "swift-package-manager"
    }}>{`Swift Package Manager`}</h3>
    <p>{`To add the package from Xcode, select `}<strong parentName="p">{`File -> Add Package Dependencies…`}</strong>{` and enter `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-concierge-ios.git`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`To add it via a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Package.swift`}</inlineCode>{` file instead, add the package to your dependencies:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`dependencies: [
    .package(url: "https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-concierge-ios.git", .upToNextMajor(from: "5.0.0")),
    .package(url: "https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-core-ios.git", .upToNextMajor(from: "5.7.0")),
    .package(url: "https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-edgeidentity-ios.git", .upToNextMajor(from: "5.0.0"))
]
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Then add the products to the target's dependencies:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`.product(name: "AEPBrandConcierge", package: "aepsdk-concierge-ios"),
.product(name: "AEPCore", package: "aepsdk-core-ios"),
.product(name: "AEPEdgeIdentity", package: "aepsdk-edgeidentity-ios"),
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "cocoapods"
    }}>{`CocoaPods`}</h3>
    <p>{`Add the following to the app's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Podfile`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-ruby"
      }}>{`pod 'AEPBrandConcierge', '~> 5.0'
pod 'AEPCore', '~> 5.7'
pod 'AEPEdgeIdentity', '~> 5.0'
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Then run `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`pod install`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "binaries"
    }}>{`Binaries`}</h3>
    <p>{`To add the XCFramework directly, run the following from the repository root:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`make archive
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`This generates `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AEPBrandConcierge.xcframework`}</inlineCode>{` under the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`build`}</inlineCode>{` folder. Drag and drop it into your app target in Xcode.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "configuration"
    }}>{`Configuration`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "step-1-register-the-brand-concierge-extension"
    }}>{`Step 1: Register the Brand Concierge extension`}</h3>
    <p>{`Import the required frameworks and register the extensions in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)`}</inlineCode>{` in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AppDelegate`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import AEPBrandConcierge
import AEPCore
import AEPEdgeIdentity
import UIKit

class AppDelegate: UIResponder, UIApplicationDelegate {
    func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions _: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
        let extensions = [
            Concierge.self,
            Identity.self
        ]

        MobileCore.registerExtensions(extensions) {
            MobileCore.configureWith(appId: "YOUR_APP_ID")
        }

        return true
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Replace `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`YOUR_APP_ID`}</inlineCode>{` with your mobile property App ID from Adobe Data Collection. For full setup instructions see the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/src/pages/home/getting-started/"
      }}>{`Adobe Experience Platform Mobile SDK getting started guide`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "step-2-validate-the-brand-concierge-configuration-keys"
    }}>{`Step 2: Validate the Brand Concierge configuration keys`}</h3>
    <p>{`If you set the Adobe Experience Platform SDK log level to trace:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`MobileCore.setLogLevel(.trace)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`you can then inspect the app logs to confirm that extension shared states are being set with the expected values.`}</p>
    <p>{`Brand Concierge expects the following keys in the Configuration shared state:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`concierge.server`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: String (server host or base domain for Concierge requests)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`concierge.configId`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: String (datastream ID)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The ECID is read from the Edge Identity shared state. Surfaces are not a Configuration key; they are supplied per session via the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`surfaces:`}</inlineCode>{` parameter on `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.wrap(...)`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.show(...)`}</inlineCode>{`, or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.present(on:...)`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`Another option for validation is to use Adobe Assurance. Refer to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/src/pages/home/getting-started/validate/"
      }}>{`Mobile SDK validation guide`}</a>{` for more information.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "optional-styling"
    }}>{`Optional styling`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "theme-injection-recommended"
    }}>{`Theme injection (recommended)`}</h3>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge chat interface can be customized by loading a theme JSON and applying it above `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.wrap(...)`}</inlineCode>{` so both the floating button and the overlay use it. The UI reads styling from the SwiftUI environment value `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`conciergeTheme`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`let theme = ConciergeThemeLoader.load(from: "theme-default", in: .main) ?? ConciergeThemeLoader.default()

var body: some View {
    Concierge.wrap(AppRootView(), surfaces: ["my-surface"], hideButton: true)
        .conciergeTheme(theme)
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`More information regarding theme customization can be found in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/edge/adobe-brand-concierge/ios/style-guide/"
      }}>{`Style guide (iOS)`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "basic-usage"
    }}>{`Basic usage`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "option-a--manual-api-call-no-floating-button"
    }}>{`Option A — Manual API call (no floating button)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use this when you want full control over where the entry point lives.`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Wrap your root content and hide the built-in button:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.wrap(AppRootView(), surfaces: ["my-surface"], hideButton: true)
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 2
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Trigger chat from your own UI:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Button("Chat") {
    Concierge.show(surfaces: ["my-surface"], title: "Concierge", subtitle: "Powered by Adobe")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.show()`}</inlineCode>{` also accepts optional parameters:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`speechCapturer`}</inlineCode>{`: A `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`SpeechCapturing`}</inlineCode>{` implementation for voice input (a default is created internally if not passed).`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`textSpeaker`}</inlineCode>{`: A `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`TextSpeaking`}</inlineCode>{` implementation for text-to-speech (off by default unless you supply one).`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{`: A callback invoked when a link is tapped in the chat. See `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "#link-handling"
        }}>{`Link Handling`}</a>{` for details.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "option-b--floating-button-built-in"
    }}>{`Option B — Floating button (built-in)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use this for a drop-in entry point:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.wrap(AppRootView(), surfaces: ["my-surface"]) // hideButton defaults to false
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`This shows a floating button in the bottom trailing corner; tapping it calls `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.show(surfaces:)`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.wrap()`}</inlineCode>{` also accepts optional parameters:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`title`}</inlineCode>{`: Title shown in the chat header.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`subtitle`}</inlineCode>{`: Subtitle shown under the title.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{`: A callback invoked when a link is tapped in the chat. See `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "#link-handling"
        }}>{`Link Handling`}</a>{` for details.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "closing-the-ui"
    }}>{`Closing the UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`Dismiss the overlay from code with:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.hide()
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "uikit-usage"
    }}>{`UIKit usage`}</h2>
    <p>{`Use this when your app is UIKit-based and you want to present Concierge from a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`UIViewController`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "present-the-chat-ui"
    }}>{`Present the chat UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.present(on:surfaces:title:subtitle:)`}</inlineCode>{` from the view controller that should host the chat:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import AEPBrandConcierge

final class MyViewController: UIViewController {
    @objc private func openChat() {
        Concierge.present(on: self, surfaces: ["my-surface"], title: "Concierge", subtitle: "Powered by Adobe")
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "dismiss-the-chat-ui"
    }}>{`Dismiss the chat UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`To dismiss the presented UI:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.hide()
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "link-handling"
    }}>{`Link Handling`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "universal-links"
    }}>{`Universal Links`}</h3>
    <p>{`To have the SDK open http/https URLs natively in your app instead of the in-app WebView, configure `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/entitlements/com_apple_developer_associated-domains"
      }}>{`Associated Domains`}</a>{` for your app and host an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`apple-app-site-association`}</inlineCode>{` file on your domain. When the domain is verified, tapping a link for that domain in the chat will navigate within your app instead of the WebView.`}</p>
    <p>{`Alternatively, use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback to intercept specific domains and handle them with custom navigation logic without requiring domain verification.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "default-behavior"
    }}>{`Default behavior`}</h3>
    <p>{`When a user taps a link in the chat, the SDK routes it through `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeLinkHandler`}</inlineCode>{` using the following flow:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Custom scheme URLs`}</strong>{` (e.g. `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`myapp://`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`mailto:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`tel:`}</inlineCode>{`) — opened immediately via the system (deep link).`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`http/https URLs`}</strong>{` — the system is first asked to open the URL as a universal link. If the host app has registered the URL's domain via `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/entitlements/com_apple_developer_associated-domains"
        }}>{`Associated Domains`}</a>{`, the app handles the navigation natively. Otherwise, the URL falls back to the in-app WebView overlay.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Default link handling flow:`}</strong>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback (if provided) -> deep link / universal link check -> WebView overlay.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "custom-link-handling"
    }}>{`Custom link handling`}</h3>
    <p>{`All three public APIs accept an optional `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` closure that is called before the SDK's default routing. Return `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` to claim the URL (the SDK takes no further action). Return `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`false`}</inlineCode>{` to let the SDK handle it normally.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`SwiftUI — `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`Concierge.wrap()`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.wrap(
    AppRootView(),
    surfaces: ["my-surface"],
    hideButton: true,
    handleLink: { url in
        if url.scheme == "myapp" {
            Concierge.hide()
            // Navigate to in-app destination
            return true
        }
        return false
    }
)
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`SwiftUI — `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`Concierge.show()`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.show(
    surfaces: ["my-surface"],
    title: "Concierge",
    subtitle: "Powered by Adobe",
    handleLink: { url in
        if url.host == "myapp.example.com" {
            Concierge.hide()
            return true
        }
        return false
    }
)
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`UIKit — `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`Concierge.present(on:)`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.present(
    on: self,
    surfaces: ["my-surface"],
    title: "Concierge",
    subtitle: "Powered by Adobe",
    handleLink: { url in
        if url.scheme == "myapp" {
            Concierge.hide()
            // Navigate using UIKit navigation
            return true
        }
        return false
    }
)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`When `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` returns `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{`, the SDK does not open the WebView overlay or perform any further link routing. When it returns `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`false`}</inlineCode>{` or is not provided, the SDK uses the default flow (deep link check -> universal link check -> WebView overlay).`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "in-app-webview-overlay-link-handling"
    }}>{`In-app WebView overlay link handling`}</h3>
    <p>{`Links clicked inside the in-app WebView overlay (for example, links on a page that has already loaded in the overlay) follow their own routing rules, independent of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`http`}</inlineCode>{` / `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`https`}</inlineCode>{` / `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`about`}</inlineCode>{` URLs`}</strong>{`: Loaded within the WebView.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Non-web schemes`}</strong>{` (for example, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`mailto:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`tel:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`sms:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`myapp://`}</inlineCode>{`): The WebView cancels the navigation and forwards the URL to the system via `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`UIApplication.open`}</inlineCode>{`, which routes it to the appropriate handler app (Mail, Phone, Messages, a custom deep-link destination, etc.).`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`No additional configuration is required for this behavior. Universal-link forwarding for in-chat links (the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` -> universal link -> WebView fallback described above) applies only to links tapped in chat messages; it is not re-evaluated for links inside an already loaded WebView page.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>

      "id": "next-steps"
    }}>{`Next steps`}</h2>

    </ul>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
